hero.p: Business is changing. I help reshape mindset and processes so that AI becomes your advantage, not a threat: less routine, more speed, stronger decisions, and a more resilient business. No buzzwords or unnecessary complexity. First consultation is free.

community.title: AI meetup - once a month
community.sub: An open meetup for business owners and anyone working with AI.

formats.title: AI meetup
formats.body: Once a month we get together at Undercover. A few short talks about what actually works, plus easy conversation over coffee. Come to listen, ask a question, or show what you're building.

meetupForm.title: Sign up for the AI meetup
meetupForm.sub: Leave your details - I'll send you the details of the next meetup.
meetupForm.name: Name *
meetupForm.note: What would you like to talk about?
meetupForm.sentSub: I'll send the details of the next meetup to your email.
meetupForm.note2: Free · we'll remind you before the meetup · no spam

club.title: AI meetup
club.sub: An open meetup once a month for business owners and people working with AI. No jargon.

pillars.title: Useful, not theoretical
pillars.desc: We talk about what actually works in practice, in plain language - no scientific terms, no promises about the distant future.

pillars.title: Real conversation
pillars.desc: Come just to listen, ask a question, or show what you're doing yourself. Meet the people working on the same things.

pillars.title: Free to attend
pillars.desc: Taking part is free, you only need to sign up. Come alone or bring a colleague.

steps.title: We talk
steps.desc: A free 30–40 minute consultation - by video or at my coworking space. What do you want to fix? What have you tried? Where are you stuck?

steps.title: A plan
steps.desc: I write down what I'd do, what isn't worth doing, and what it costs. You decide if we keep going.

steps.title: We build
steps.desc: I implement, train, or work alongside your team. Always on your real tasks.

steps.title: I hand it over
steps.desc: The result stays with you and your team. Recordings, instructions, and a month of support included.

services.title: How I can be useful
services.sub: Three ways to work together. We always start with a free consultation.
services.grantNote: Indicative prices - every project is scoped on the free consultation. Most work qualifies for EIS grants, so the state can cover up to 70% of the cost.

lines.title: Sort out your processes and mindset
items: We look at how work flows in your company
We simplify and cut the extra
We shift the mindset so AI becomes a helper, not a problem

lines.title: Adding AI to your processes
items: Deep dive into your tasks and processes
I pick AI tools to fit your tasks
Training your team to work with the new tools

lines.title: One-on-one training
items: Foundations of business thinking in the AI era

funding.title: The state co-pays for AI and automation
funding.sub: Estonia gives back part of the money for work we already do. Amounts for 2026.
funding.disclaimer: Terms change - check eis.ee before applying. Baseline requirement: an Estonian company with 2-year average revenue of at least €50,000.

grants.title: Buy and set up ready-made software
grants.fit: A good fit if you need to buy and set up a ready tool: CRM, an assistant, a bot, accounting. The fastest and simplest first step.

grants.title: Process automation
grants.fit: A fit for custom builds: AI assistants, office routine automation, working with data. Most of our projects fit here.

grants.title: Digitalisation roadmap
grants.urgency: Best first step before a bigger grant
grants.fit: A small grant to draw up a plan together with a consultant. A handy entry ticket - it's easier to apply for a bigger grant afterwards.

grants.title: Applied research
grants.fit: For serious projects that build their own model. Applications are prepared in advance, 2–3 months ahead.

help.title: I'll help with the application
help.body: Half the job is describing the project the right way so it fits the EIS criteria. I help with that.

grantCheck.title: See which grant your company qualifies for
grantCheck.sub: Enter a registration code - I'll show what's available to you under EIS.
grantCheck.errorGeneric: Couldn't get a response. Try again.
grantCheck.disclaimer: This is a rough estimate. The final call is EIS's.
grantCheck.takesAFewMinutes: This request may take a few minutes - we pull data from Inforegister and compute the result.

leadMagnet.title: Not ready for a consultation yet? Get the AI Grants 2026 guide
leadMagnet.sub: A short, plain-language PDF: which Estonian AI & automation grants exist in 2026 and how to qualify - no consultation required.
bullets: All 2026 EIS grants on one page, in plain words
Who qualifies and the typical amounts
The first step to take for each one
leadMagnet.note: One email with the guide · no spam · unsubscribe anytime
leadMagnet.sentSub: Download it below - I've also noted your email so I can send updates if the rules change.
leadMagnet.err: Something went wrong. Write to me directly:

clients.title: Who I've worked with
clients.sub: A few of the companies I've helped.

testimonials.title: What clients say
testimonials.sub: A few words from the companies I've worked with.

items.quote: What used to take our team two days now takes ten minutes. The tool reads a tender, tells us whether it fits, and drafts the paperwork - and Sergei made sure we actually understood how it works.
items.name: Our tools

items.quote: Based on report data analysis, reports our analysts built by hand for days are now ready in minutes, formatted to our template. The rollout was calm and practical - no hype, just working software.
items.name: Kent Hanson

items.quote: Our laser cutting machine and our accounting finally talk to each other. No more manual order entry, no more invoicing delays. Sergei found the saving and built it without disrupting the floor.
items.name: Aleksei Saareväli

items.quote: After creating new AI tools our reporting services over all of Estonia, reports, analytics, procurement, and communication with Roads registry became much faster and more efficient. Sergei is a great partner to work with - he understands the needs of the business and finds the right solution.
items.name: Andrei Anisimov

cases.title: What this looks like in practice
cases.sub: A few projects. Real problems, real numbers.

items.title: Steel structures factory
items.problem: The machine ran separately from the accounting system. Operators typed in every order by hand - which meant mistakes and delays in invoicing.
items.solution: I connected the machine and the accounting system directly. Data flows on its own: jobs in, actuals back.

items.title: Forsta
items.problem: Analysts spent days building client reports by hand - copying, formatting, cross-checking.
items.solution: A program builds the report from the client's template on its own and hands it over for review.

items.title: Public procurement assistant
items.problem: One tender is 50–200 pages of text. Reading and preparing it took days.
items.solution: A tool reads the tender documents, checks whether it fits the company, and drafts the paperwork.

roi.title: Let's estimate the savings
roi.sub: A rough estimate of what automating routine work could give back. Back-of-envelope.
roi.estimate: A rough estimate. Real numbers come out of a consultation.

about.title: Who's running this
about.role: I help businesses tidy up processes and put AI to work
about.intro: I'm Sergei Zahharenko. For over a decade I've built software for companies that count every euro - and now I bring AI into their day-to-day work, not their slide decks. I take on a handful of projects at a time so each one gets done properly.
items: Over 10 years building and rolling out software systems.
I build AI into the real work of companies - not into pretty slide decks.
Background in finance and manufacturing - places that count every euro.
I speak Russian, Estonian, and English.
cardItems: AI isn't a trendy toy - it's a new work tool.
It doesn't replace people; it takes the routine off your plate and helps you decide faster.
The winner isn't the one who talks about AI the most, but the one who started using it for real first.

items.q: How much does the first consultation cost?
items.a: Nothing. The first 30–40 minute consultation is free. We figure out the task and whether I can help.

items.q: I'm completely new to AI. Is that OK?
items.a: Yes. We start with your real tasks, not 'what is AI' theory. No preparation needed.
Yes. I take on a few projects at a time so I can do them well. The first consultation is always free.
Loud talks for the hype, marketing decks about 'AI strategy', and '10 prompts that will change your life' posts. I'm about real work.

contact.title: Tell me what you're working on
contact.sub: I read everything. Reply within 24 hours.

form.name: Name *
form.sentSub: I'll get back to you within 24 hours.

cookie.text: This site uses cookies for analytics.

sections.title: 1. Who we are
sections.body: TarKratt - AI practice, personal training, and consulting. Run by Sergei Zahharenko. Part of Undercover, Tallinn, Estonia. Contact: acrashik@gmail.com

sections.title: 2. What data we collect
sections.body: Via contact form: name, email, company, task description. Via cookies (with consent): anonymous analytics.

sections.title: 3. How we use data
sections.body: Only to respond to your inquiry. We do not share with third parties or use for advertising.

sections.title: 4. Legal basis (GDPR)
sections.body: Processing based on consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) and legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR).

sections.title: 5. Retention
sections.body: Form data - no more than 2 years. Deletion on request at any time.

sections.title: 6. Your rights
sections.body: Access, correction, deletion, portability, withdrawal of consent. Contact: acrashik@gmail.com

sections.title: 7. Cookies
sections.body: Used only after your explicit consent. Withdrawal - via banner or clearing cookies.

sections.title: 8. Supervisory authority
sections.body: Andmekaitse Inspektsioon (Estonian Data Protection Inspectorate): aki.ee
